summary: Apple has struck a deal with Taboola, the company best known for chumbox ads, to sell native advertising in Apple News and Apple Stocks apps. Gruber notes that Apple News ads already looked like Taboola-style chumbox ads before the deal, making the partnership unsurprising. He documents the poor ad experience in Apple News with screen recordings showing highly repetitive ads while reading a Rolling Stone article. While acknowledging this isn't good news, he sardonically suggests the partnership is unlikely to make the already-low ad quality any worse.
key_points: Taboola, known for chumbox ads at the bottom of web articles, will now power native advertising in Apple News and Apple Stocks in every market
Eric Seufert argues the partnership isn't hypocritical from a privacy standpoint since Taboola ads are contextually targeted, not personally targeted
Gruber demonstrates that Apple News ads are already repetitive and low-quality, with single ads appearing four times in one article scroll
The irony that Rolling Stone is already a Taboola partner on the web, and the chumbox content there matches what Apple News already shows
Even with personalized ads enabled, Gruber's Apple News ad experience is dominated by repetitive, chumbox-style placements
money_quotes: If you told me that the ads in Apple News have been sold by Taboola for the last few years, I'd have said, 'Oh, that makes sense.'
Because the ads in Apple News — at least the ones I see — already look like chumbox Taboola ads. Even worse, they're incredibly repetitious.
So while I don't think it's good news that Apple is partnering with Taboola, I don't expect it to make any discernible difference in the ad quality or frequency. Maybe it will improve the variety?
Want brash, garish advertising plastered all over the web? Reject ads personalization. Want relevant, informed advertising? Embrace ads personalization.
I do have 'Personalized Ads' enabled in Settings → Privacy & Security → Apple Advertising. So I should be seeing the best ads Apple has to offer in Apple News.
key_insight: Apple's partnership with Taboola is less a fall from grace than a formalization of the chumbox-quality advertising already plaguing Apple News.
